title: no matching entries in passwd file - podman generate kube
body:
/kind bug

Trying to generate a pod definition for a manually generated pod:

```
$ $ podman --log-level=debug generate kube pod-httpd.ibox.local
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /var/lib/containers/users/httpd.ibox.local/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver vfs                       
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/lib/containers/users/httpd.ibox.local/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /var/lib/containers/users/httpd.ibox.local/run 
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /var/lib/containers/users/httpd.ibox.local/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/pods/20000/libpod/tmp     
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/lib/containers/users/httpd.ibox.local/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "vfs"   
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] Looking in container for user: 1001          
DEBU[0000] Looking in container for user: user:user     
ERRO[0000] no matching entries in passwd file  
```

Further debug info:

```

comments.body: Any chance you can attempt this against master branch.  I remember someone (Perhaps me) fixing an issue similar to this one.
I haven't yet been able to build master on EL7, any way there can be a copr for that?
@rhatdan I did some testing with a custom built version of a 1.6.3 el7 rpm and found that it failed with `--user root:root` and `--user 0:0` but it succeeded with `--user root` and `--user 0`.
This is fixed in master branch.

```
$ /bin/podman pod create --name p1
b70f3196b765eb55de9586a714ff0d6d3bf04b002b333edea4566682bf851c4e
$ ./bin/podman create --pod p1 --user root:root fedora sleep 1000
686c86e1573cceecfc3184735c335696f2427e387eb918d0673c0c6a69ade92a
$ bin/podman pod create --name p1
$ ./bin/podman generate kube p1
# Generation of Kubernetes YAML is still under development!
#
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-1.6.4-dev
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2019-12-04T04:03:36Z"
  labels:
    app: p1
  name: p1
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - sleep
    - "1000"
    env:
    - name: PATH
      value: /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
    - name: TERM
      value: xterm
    - name: HOSTNAME
      value: p1
    - name: container
      value: podman
    - name: DISTTAG
      value: f31-updates-candidatecontainer
    - name: FGC
      value: f31-updates-candidate
    - name: FBR
      value: f31-updates-candidate
    image: docker.io/library/fedora:latest
    name: crankybose
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
      capabilities: {}
      privileged: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
      runAsGroup: 0
      runAsUser: 0
    workingDir: /
status: {}
```

Is this fixed? I am getting the same error.

Packager    : Red Hat, Inc. <http://bugzilla.redhat.com/bugzilla>
Vendor      : Red Hat, Inc.
URL         : https://podman.io/
Summary     : Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images
Description :
podman (Pod Manager) is a fully featured container engine that is a simple
daemonless tool.  podman provides a Docker-CLI comparable command line that
eases the transition from other container engines and allows the management of
pods, containers and images.  Simply put: alias docker=podman.
Most podman commands can be run as a regular user, without requiring
additional privileges.

podman uses Buildah(1) internally to create container images.
Both tools share image (not container) storage, hence each can use or
manipulate images (but not containers) created by the other.

Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images
libpod Simple management tool for pods, containers and images```
